doctor_turn: What brought you in today?
patient_turn: Yeah, so uh, just randomly like 2 days ago I just started having this chest pain when I'm breathing and it's just been getting worse and then because of that, I'm finding that I'm very short of breath as well, so I just got really concerned.

doctor_turn: When did the chest pain start?
patient_turn: So, there was some discomfort like just 2 days ago, but then just yesterday it started getting very painful when I was breathing. So yeah, I would say 1 day ago.

doctor_turn: Were you doing anything at the time when the chest pain started? Like did you get in a car accident maybe? Or any trauma to the chest or anything you can think of?
patient_turn: No, not really. I was just at home and then I noticed that when, I like, take a deep breath in, it's just, like, a very sharp pain. It's kind of like in my right side of the chest and it feels like a stabbing pain.

doctor_turn: Does the pain come on at any other points or does it go away?
patient_turn: Uh, it's mostly, yeah, I feel the sharp pain when I breathe in.

doctor_turn: Has there been anything you've tried that's made the pain better?
patient_turn: Not really, just more shallow breathing, and it hurts less with that, but that's about it.

doctor_turn: Are there any positions that help make the pain any better like leaning forward or laying down?
patient_turn: Not really, it's uh, it's pretty much the same, I think.

doctor_turn: You mentioned you had shortness of breath as well. When does this come on?
patient_turn: Since yesterday it's been pretty constant and it's more like, Uh, just uh, I'm really having trouble getting any deep breaths in, so I feel like I'm having to just breathe faster and more shallow.

doctor_turn: Has anything like this happened to you before?
Have you been experiencing any headaches?

patient_turn: I have been having a little bit of a cough, but that's it, like, it kind of started today, but it's nothing too significant.

Have you had a sore throat?
How about a loss of taste or a loss of smell?

Do you drink alcohol?

Any concerns right now or anything like that?
patient_turn: No, uh, we've been pretty safe and we've both been tested before and everything, so I'm not really concerned.

patient_turn: No, I think my sister, she has asthma, but that's about it.

doctor_turn: Any cancers that run in the family?

OK, so it sounds like you had this uncomfortable feeling in your chest about two days ago, but the pain really came on yesterday and it's really painful when you have a deep deep breath. So, in conjunction with your symptoms of not being able to take a deep breath and being short of breath, it sounds like you possibly have what's called a pneumothorax and so we'll do a physical exam and do a chest X-ray as well to examine this and check vitals as well to make sure that you're work of breathing is OK and we can do some blood work and go from there.
patient_turn: OK, yeah, sounds good. Should be like, worried at all?

doctor_turn: I think right now, you're speaking in full sentences and you're not short of breath at rest, typically, when this this happens, if it's small enough, it'll usually resolve on its own and so we'll get some more information from the imaging and the lab results, but it's reassuring that you haven't been getting worse since it happened.
patient_turn: OK, alright, sounds good. Thank you so much.
